name: log_food
description: Log parsed food items with their nutritional content.

reasoning.description: IMAGE inputs ONLY: before listing foods, briefly reason (<=600 chars) — identify each visible item, estimate its WEIGHT IN GRAMS using visible scale references (plate/bowl diameter, cutlery, a hand, standard packaging), then note the calorie density you will apply. Leave empty / omit entirely for text input.

emoji.description: Single emoji that best represents this specific food or dish. Always pick the MOST SPECIFIC glyph — e.g. for fish/cod/salmon use 🐟, for beef/steak use 🥩, for chicken use 🍗, for eggs use 🥚, for pasta use 🍝, for salad use 🥗, for avocado use 🥑. NEVER output 🍽️ (the generic plate) unless the dish genuinely has no better match. Output exactly one Unicode emoji — no text, no multiple glyphs.

low_confidence.description: true when: (1) this row is a decomposed add-in of a variable-preparation food (e.g. milk in 'americano with milk') and the user should verify the amount, OR (2) this row was read from a nutrition label where one or more digits were hard to distinguish — the user should retake the photo for a clearer read. false for all other rows.

request_text.description: The user's own wording for JUST this item, spelling/grammar corrected, same language (never translated), proper nouns preserved verbatim. Omit when there is no user-typed text for this item (e.g. an image with no caption).

library_name.description: EXACT ingredient name copied VERBATIM from the LIBRARY block when this food matches one of the listed ingredients. Omit when the food isn't in the list.

variant.description: EXACT form label copied VERBATIM from that ingredient's bracketed label list — the form the food was eaten in. Omit when the ingredient is listed without brackets or the food isn't in the list.

readiness.description: Preparation state of the food AS EATEN. Always set when determinable, even for foods not in the library.

refusal_reason.description: Set when foods=[] for a STRUCTURAL reason: 'barcode_only' = user photographed a barcode/back-of-package; 'zero_nutrition' = real food/drink recognised but genuinely calorie-free (black tea, plain water, diet soda). Omit for non-food text or normal parsing.
